perturbation.original_text: Evaluation Material shall not include, however, information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in violation of this letter agreement; (ii) was available to the Receiving Party on a nonconfidential basis prior to its disclosure by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives; (iii) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a nonconfidential basis from a person other than the Disclosing Party or its Representatives who is not otherwise bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives, or is otherwise not known to the Receiving Party to be under an obligation to the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives not to transmit the information to the Receiving Party; or (iv) was independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Evaluation Material shall not include, however, information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives in violation of this letter agreement; (ii) was available to the Receiving Party on a confidential basis prior to its disclosure by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives; (iii) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a nonconfidential basis from a person other than the Disclosing Party or its Representatives who is not otherwise bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives, or is otherwise not known to the Receiving Party to be under an obligation to the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives not to transmit the information to the Receiving Party; or (iv) was independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "was available to the Receiving Party on a *nonconfidential* basis prior to its disclosure" has been changed to "was available to the Receiving Party on a *confidential* basis prior to its disclosure". This creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, information available to the Receiving Party on a nonconfidential basis *before* disclosure by the Disclosing Party was *excluded* from the definition of 'Evaluation Material'. Now, if the information was already available on a *confidential* basis, it *is still excluded*, which raises the question of why this confidential data would still be exempted from confidentiality and creates confusion. It undermines the overall aim of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the immediately succeeding paragraph, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party (i) except as required by law, rule or regulation, shall keep all Evaluation Material confidential, shall not disclose or reveal any Evaluation Material to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Evaluation Material for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction and shall cause those persons to observe the terms of this letter agreement; (ii) shall not use Evaluation Material for any purpose other than in connection with its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or the consummation of the Proposed Transaction in a manner that the Disclosing Party has approved; and (iii) except as required by law, rule or regulation, shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction, which Representatives it shall cause to observe the terms of this agreement,) any information about the Proposed Transaction, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Evaluation Material has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'nonconfidential' to 'confidential' creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text excludes information already available on a nonconfidential basis, meaning such information is not subject to the confidentiality obligations. The changed text excludes information available on a confidential basis prior to disclosure. However, the contradicted text explicitly states that the Receiving Party shall keep all Evaluation Material confidential, including not disclosing it. By excluding information already available on a confidential basis, the change creates an inconsistency because such confidential information, while being excluded from the definition of 'Evaluation Material', should still be protected under the confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted text, but the exclusion undermines this obligation.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this letter agreement, (i) "Representative" shall mean, as to any person, its directors, officers, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys and accountants) and debt and equity financing sources and their advisors and Representatives (but shall not include any debt and equity financing sources that enter into a confidentiality agreement reasonably <PAGE> 2 acceptable to the Disclosing Party, which either names Disclosing Party as a third party beneficiary or to which Disclosing Party is made a party, and an executed copy of which is provided to Disclosing Party); and (ii) "person" shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporation, company, partnership, other entity or individual.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this letter agreement, (i) "Representative" shall mean, as to any person, its directors, officers, and employees only; and (ii) "person" shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporation, company, partnership, other entity or individual.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowing the definition of 'Representative' to exclude "agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys and accountants) and debt and equity financing sources and their advisors and Representatives", it directly contradicts the section that holds the Receiving Party secondarily responsible for breaches of the agreement by its Representatives. This narrowed definition implies those actors are no longer included in the scope of 'Representative', reducing the Receiving Party's responsibility for their actions related to Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by it and secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Representative' to exclude agents, advisors, financial advisors, attorneys, accountants, debt and equity financing sources, and their representatives. This contradicts the section stating that the Receiving Party is 'secondarily responsible for any breach of the terms of this letter agreement by its Representatives' because the changed definition reduces the scope of individuals for whose actions the Receiving Party is responsible, creating a direct conflict between the two sections. It adheres to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the conflicting definition changes responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives are requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law or regulation (including, without limitation, any rule, regulation or policy statement of any national securities exchange, market or automated quotation system on which any of the Receiving Party's securities are listed or quoted) or by legal process to disclose any Evaluation Material or any other information concerning the Disclosing Party or the Proposed Transaction, the Receiving Party shall provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Disclosing Party (i) to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, (ii) to consult with the Receiving Party with respect to the Receiving Party's taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or (iii) to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law or regulation (including, without limitation, any rule, regulation or policy statement of any national securities exchange, market or automated quotation system on which any of the Receiving Party's securities are listed or quoted) or by legal process to disclose any Evaluation Material or any other information concerning the Disclosing Party or the Proposed Transaction, the Receiving Party is not obligated to provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement in order to enable the Disclosing Party (i) to seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, (ii) to consult with the Receiving Party with respect to the Receiving Party's taking steps to resist or narrow the scope of such request or legal process, or (iii) to waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this letter agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "shall provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice" to "is not obligated to provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice" creates a direct contradiction. Initially, the Receiving Party had a clear obligation to inform the Disclosing Party about any legal requirement to disclose Evaluation Material, which allowed the Disclosing Party to take protective measures. Now, this obligation is removed, preventing the Disclosing Party from protecting its confidential information. It directly undermines the confidentiality intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to either party hereto, each party shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the other party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this letter agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Receiving Party's obligation to notify the Disclosing Party of any legal requirement to disclose Evaluation Material. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that each party is entitled to equitable relief if the other party breaches the agreement. By removing the notification obligation, the Receiving Party could potentially disclose confidential information without the Disclosing Party's knowledge, which would constitute a breach. The original text ensured the Disclosing Party had a chance to seek a protective order. The new text removes this protection, creating a direct conflict and undermining the ability of the Disclosing Party to seek equitable relief against a breach as stated in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: If either party hereto shall determine that it does not wish to proceed with the Proposed Transaction, such party shall promptly advise the other party of that decision. In that case, or in the event that the Disclosing Party, in its sole discretion, so requests or the Proposed Transaction is not consummated by the Receiving Party, the Receiving party shall, upon the Disclosing Party's written request, promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material, and, at the Receiving Party's election, return or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of the Receiving Party) all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof, including any electronic or computer file copies, or based thereon in the Receiving Party's possession or in the possession of any Representative of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: If either party hereto shall determine that it does not wish to proceed with the Proposed Transaction, such party may or may not advise the other party of that decision at the party's discretion. In that case, or in the event that the Disclosing Party, in its sole discretion, so requests or the Proposed Transaction is not consummated by the Receiving Party, the Receiving party shall, upon the Disclosing Party's verbal request, at their discretion, deliver to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material, and, at the Receiving Party's election, return or not destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be certified by a non authorized Representative of the Receiving Party) all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof, including any electronic or computer file copies, or based thereon in the Receiving Party's possession or in the possession of any Representative of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall promptly advise" to "may or may not advise at the party's discretion" regarding advising that a party will not continue with the proposed transaction, conflicts with the overall goal of an open evaluation and negotiation. Likewise, changing "Disclosing Party's written request" to "Disclosing Party's verbal request, at their discretion" as well as the option "return or not destroy" the 'Evaluation Material', it introduces ambiguity and potential loss of control for the Disclosing Party over its confidential information, undermining the contract's core purpose and contradicting earlier clauses about maintaining confidentiality. The change of "authorized Representative of the Receiving Party" to a "non authorized Representative of the Receiving Party" creates uncertainty of the actual person ensuring the Evaluation Materials are properly destroyed, it introduces ambiguity in the reliability of the destruction verification, contradicting the aim of secure disposal of confidential data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the immediately succeeding paragraph, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party (i) except as required by law, rule or regulation, shall keep all Evaluation Material confidential, shall not disclose or reveal any Evaluation Material to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Evaluation Material for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction and shall cause those persons to observe the terms of this letter agreement; (ii) shall not use Evaluation Material for any purpose other than in connection with its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or the consummation of the Proposed Transaction in a manner that the Disclosing Party has approved; and (iii) except as required by law, rule or regulation, shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction, which Representatives it shall cause to observe the terms of this agreement,) any information about the Proposed Transaction, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Evaluation Material has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions with the contradicted text. The original text states that if a party does not wish to proceed with the transaction, they 'shall promptly advise' the other party. The changed text alters this to 'may or may not advise at the party's discretion,' directly conflicting with the expectation of open communication and potentially hindering the evaluation process outlined in the contradicted text, which emphasizes keeping evaluation material confidential and controlled. Furthermore, changing 'Disclosing Party's written request' to 'Disclosing Party's verbal request, at their discretion', weakens the Disclosing Party's control over the return of Evaluation Material, again contrasting the intended confidentiality as stated in the contradicted text. Changing 'authorized Representative of the Receiving Party' to a 'non authorized Representative of the Receiving Party' contradicts the aim of secure disposal of confidential data, which is at odds with the contradicted text's emphasis on maintaining confidentiality. These changes create ambiguities and weaken the safeguards intended to protect the Disclosing Party's information, creating a strong contradiction with the contradicted text.
